commands.review_summary:
The branch routes Mattermost send attachment-shaped parameters through the existing upload path, threads workspace media access, adds fail-closed local upload handling, and expands focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +159, Tests +350. Total +509 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main only forwards params.media from the Mattermost send action, so filePath and structured attachments are ignored before the existing upload layer; the linked issue also includes redacted live evidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed for the fail-closed compatibility decision and live Mattermost proof waiver; no narrow automated repair was identified.

target.title: fix(mattermost): route send attachments through upload
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes Mattermost message sends that reported success while dropping `filePath` / attachment media.
- Routes supported single attachment sources through the Mattermost upload path: `filePath`, `path`, `fileUrl`, `mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls` / `media_urls`, and structured `attachments[]`.
- Fails closed for local/path-like media upload errors while preserving the existing HTTP(S) text+URL fallback.
- Threads workspace/media access through Mattermost outbound send paths so relative workspace media can be read by the existing media loader.

## Linked context

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87930

Related: Mattermost outbound send/action attachment handling.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes, maintainer triage/fix lane.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost no longer reports `ok` after silently dropping supported local/path attachment sources; unsupported buffer/base64 or multi-attachment payloads are rejected explicitly.
- Real environment tested: local macOS focused Vitest lane before the final lint-shape amendment; Linux remote changed gate through Crabbox/Azure for final lint/typecheck.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 shards / 163 tests; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; Crabbox/Azure run `run_518f52579c7f` on lease `cbx_204afb03915d` passed core/extension typecheck and core lint, then failed only on unrelated current-main Microsoft Foundry extension lint outside this PR. That Microsoft Foundry lint is now fixed on `origin/main`; follow-up broad proof is currently blocked by runner infrastructure (`blacksmith-testbox` rsync/SSH failures and direct Crabbox Azure active lease limit `9/8`).
- Observed result after fix: Mattermost send options now include upload-required local media handling and retain HTTP(S) fallback behavior.
- What was not tested: live Mattermost server with a real token.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: final local rerun was blocked by shared `node_modules` drift in the linked worktree (`@rolldown/binding-darwin-arm64` missing and `.bin/tsgo` missing); broad changed gate rerun is currently blocked by runner infrastructure, not a known Mattermost failure.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue reproduction showed `filePath` / attachments were accepted but not uploaded.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/media/load-options.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Mattermost send action tests for `filePath`, `path`, `fileUrl`, `mediaUrls`, structured `attachments[]`, unsupported payload rejection, multi-attachment rejection, remote fallback preservation, and workspace media access.
- Mattermost outbound payload/sendMedia tests for local media upload-required behavior.
- Shared outbound media source widening tests for `media_urls` and structured attachment sources.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Mattermost accepted attachment fields but did not route them to upload, so sends could succeed without delivering the file.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, media read/upload error handling now fails closed for local Mattermost attachments.

What is the highest-risk area?

Preserving remote HTTP(S) media fallback while failing local media drops.

How is that risk mitigated?

The code gates upload-required behavior to non-HTTP media and has focused tests for both local and remote paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review; broad changed gate needs a clean rerun once Testbox sync or direct Crabbox capacity recovers.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Runner infrastructure currently blocks final `check:changed`; live Mattermost proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed; final autoreview is clean.
